nodes.scope: Root cluster for vision-language-action robotics research: foundation policies, action heads, manipulation, locomotion, perception, reasoning, world models, sim/data, efficiency, navigation, evaluation. Structural root only; does not match titles directly.
Cluster of VLA foundation policies and cross-embodiment foundation models. Structural node only; specific generalist/cross-embodiment leaves carry the matching.

Single-embodiment or single-body-class generalist VLA policies that condition a transformer on language and vision. Excludes cross-embodiment foundation models (see vla.foundation.cross_embodiment_fm) and dual-system architectures (see vla.foundation.dual_system_vla).
Foundation policies trained jointly across many robot embodiments (arms, mobile bases, humanoids). Excludes single-embodiment generalist VLAs (see vla.foundation.vla_generalist).

Two-system VLA architectures combining a slow VLM planner (System 2) with a fast control policy (System 1). Excludes monolithic VLAs (see vla.foundation.vla_generalist) and pure VLM grounding without a low-level policy (see vla.foundation.vlm_grounded_policy).

Methods that ground a frozen VLM into action via affordance keypoints, code-as-policies, or value maps without training a dedicated action head. Excludes trained VLA policies (see vla.foundation.vla_generalist).
Cluster for VLA action-head designs (tokenization, flow, diffusion, autoregressive, continuous regression). Structural node; leaves carry the matching.

Discrete action tokenization schemes: bin tokens, BPE-for-actions, frequency/FAST tokens, learned codebooks. Excludes continuous generative heads (see vla.action.flow_matching / vla.action.diffusion_policy) and regression heads (see vla.action.continuous_regression_head).

Flow-matching / rectified-flow / mean-flow action heads that learn continuous transport from noise to actions. Excludes score-based denoising heads (see vla.action.diffusion_policy) and autoregressive action prediction (see vla.action.autoregressive_action).

Score/denoising action heads conditioned on observation tokens. Excludes flow-matching heads (see vla.action.flow_matching) and continuous regression heads (see vla.action.continuous_regression_head).
Autoregressive next-token action prediction over a discrete action vocabulary. Excludes diffusion (see vla.action.diffusion_policy) and flow-matching (see vla.action.flow_matching) heads.

MSE/MLP action heads that regress continuous joint or end-effector targets directly. Excludes generative heads (see vla.action.diffusion_policy, vla.action.flow_matching) and discrete action tokens (see vla.action.action_tokenization).
Cluster for policy-learning paradigms: imitation, residual, inverse dynamics, robot RL. Structural node only.

Behavior cloning and demonstration-driven supervised policy learning. Excludes RL-based policy training (see vla.policy.robot_rl) and residual corrections atop a base policy (see vla.policy.residual_policy).

Policies trained to output a corrective residual on top of a base controller or pretrained policy. Excludes from-scratch imitation (see vla.policy.imitation_learning) and full RL fine-tuning (see vla.policy.robot_rl.vla_post_training_rl).
Inverse-dynamics models that label actions from observed (video) state transitions for downstream policy training. Excludes forward dynamics / world models (see vla.world.world_model_dynamics).
Cluster for reinforcement learning on robot policies (online, offline, VLA post-training). Structural node only; L3 leaves carry matching.

On-robot online RL with human-in-the-loop or autonomous resets. Excludes offline RL from logged data (see vla.policy.robot_rl.offline_robot_rl) and post-training a pretrained VLA (see vla.policy.robot_rl.vla_post_training_rl).

Offline RL on robot logs / demonstration replay using Cal-QL, IQL, Q-Transformer-style critics. Excludes online RL (see vla.policy.robot_rl.online_robot_rl) and pure imitation (see vla.policy.imitation_learning).
RL fine-tuning of pretrained VLA policies (PA-RL, iRe-VLA, GRPO-for-VLA). Excludes RL from scratch on a single robot (see online/offline_robot_rl).
Cluster for manipulation sub-families (dexterous, bimanual, mobile, contact-rich, tactile). RETIRED as router: this cluster never matches directly; specific sub-families carry routing.

Multi-finger dexterous grasping and in-hand manipulation. Excludes bimanual two-arm coordination (see vla.manipulation.bimanual_manipulation) and tactile sensing alone (see vla.manipulation.tactile_sensing).
Two-arm coordinated manipulation (ALOHA-class). Excludes single-arm dexterous tasks (see vla.manipulation.dexterous_manipulation) and mobile bases (see vla.manipulation.mobile_manipulation).

Wheeled/legged mobile bases coordinating navigation with arm manipulation. Excludes pure navigation (see vla.navigation.vision_language_navigation) and stationary bimanual setups (see vla.manipulation.bimanual_manipulation).

High-force contact-rich industrial assembly (peg-in-hole, gear meshing). Excludes general grasping (see vla.manipulation.dexterous_manipulation) and pure tactile readout work (see vla.manipulation.tactile_sensing).
Tactile / haptic sensing hardware and tactile representation learning. Excludes contact-rich control policies that consume tactile signals (see vla.manipulation.contact_rich_assembly).
Cluster for locomotion: humanoid whole-body, legged, loco-manipulation. Structural node only.

Humanoid whole-body control (WBC) for full-body motion tracking and balance. Excludes pure legged locomotion without an upper body (see vla.locomotion.legged_locomotion) and loco-manipulation on legs (see vla.locomotion.loco_manipulation).

Quadruped / biped legged locomotion (parkour, rough terrain). Excludes humanoid whole-body (see vla.locomotion.humanoid_whole_body) and locomotion combined with manipulation (see vla.locomotion.loco_manipulation).

Joint locomotion + manipulation control on legged platforms. Excludes wheeled mobile manipulation (see vla.manipulation.mobile_manipulation) and pure locomotion (see vla.locomotion.legged_locomotion).
Cluster for robotic perception representations: 3D, gaussian splats, affordance, visual pretraining. Structural node only.

3D scene representations for robot policies: voxels, point clouds, NeRF features (non-gaussian). Excludes gaussian-splat-specific methods (see vla.perception.gaussian_splat_robotics) and 2D affordance grounding (see vla.perception.affordance_grounding).
3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) used as scene representation or simulator for robot policies. Excludes non-gaussian 3D representations (see vla.perception.representation_3d).
Affordance keypoint / value-map grounding from VLMs for downstream manipulation. Excludes general visual pretraining (see vla.perception.visual_pretraining_for_robots).

Visual encoders pretrained for robot manipulation (R3M, VC-1, Theia). Excludes 3D scene encoders (see vla.perception.representation_3d) and affordance grounding (see vla.perception.affordance_grounding).
Cluster for embodied reasoning: CoT, hierarchical planning, error recovery. Structural node only.

Chain-of-thought reasoning inlined inside the VLA action loop (ECoT, CoT-VLA, RT-H). Excludes high-level multi-step planning (see vla.reasoning.hierarchical_planning) and replanning on failure (see vla.reasoning.error_recovery_replanning).

Hierarchical task planners that produce sub-goals consumed by a low-level policy (SayCan-style). Excludes inline CoT inside the action head (see vla.reasoning.embodied_cot) and reactive error recovery (see vla.reasoning.error_recovery_replanning).
Failure detection and replanning during execution (REFLECT, DoReMi, OLAF). Excludes offline failure analysis benchmarks (see vla.eval.failure_analysis_robotics).
Cluster for world models in robotics: latent dynamics, video generation, neural simulators. Structural node only.

Latent / pixel dynamics world models trained for planning or representation in robotics (Dreamer-class, GR-1/GR-2, iVideoGPT). Excludes large pixel video generators used as data (see vla.world.video_generation_for_robotics) and learned simulators as drop-in environments (see vla.world.neural_simulator).

Pixel video generators producing robot futures used as plans, augmentation, or supervision (UniPi, Cosmos, RoboDreamer, GR00T Dreams). Excludes compact latent-dynamics world models (see vla.world.world_model_dynamics).

Learned neural simulators used as drop-in physics environments (1X World Model, Genie 2 used as sim). Excludes representational world models (see vla.world.world_model_dynamics) and traditional sim-to-real pipelines (see vla.sim_data.sim_to_real).
Cluster for simulation, real-to-sim, data collection, synthetic data, and VLA scaling laws. Structural node only.

Domain randomization and policy transfer from simulation to physical robots. Excludes building digital twins from real scans (see vla.sim_data.real_to_sim) and learned neural simulators (see vla.world.neural_simulator).

Reconstructing simulation assets from real-world scans / URDFs (URDFormer, RoboCasa, DigitalCousins). Excludes sim-to-real transfer (see vla.sim_data.sim_to_real) and synthetic-trajectory generation (see vla.sim_data.synthetic_data_generation).
Hardware/software for teleoperated robot data collection (UMI, DexCap, HATO, AnyTeleop). Excludes synthetic data generation in simulation (see vla.sim_data.synthetic_data_generation).

Procedural / generative synthetic trajectory generation for VLA training (RoboGen, DexMimicGen, GR00T-Mimic). Excludes physical teleoperated data collection (see vla.sim_data.data_collection_systems).
Empirical scaling-law studies for VLA / robot foundation models. Excludes generic LLM scaling work and individual model releases.
Cluster for VLA efficiency: acceleration, async inference. Catch-all cluster; never matches directly (leaves carry routing).
Catch-all for VLA inference acceleration (quantization, distillation, pruning, parallel decoding). Excludes async / chunked inference scheduling (see vla.efficiency.async_inference).
Asynchronous / chunked action prediction for low-latency control (action chunking, RTC). Excludes weight-level acceleration (see vla.efficiency.vla_acceleration).
Cluster for navigation: vision-language navigation, autonomous driving end-to-end. Structural node only.

Indoor/outdoor vision-language navigation policies (VLN). Excludes end-to-end self-driving (see vla.navigation.autonomous_driving_e2e) and mobile manipulation (see vla.manipulation.mobile_manipulation).
End-to-end autonomous driving policies (UniAD, DriveVLM, EMMA). Excludes indoor navigation (see vla.navigation.vision_language_navigation).
Cluster for VLA evaluation: safety, benchmarks, failure analysis. Structural node only.

Safe RL and ISO-compliant safety constraints for physical robots. Excludes AI agent safety (see ai.safety.agent_safety) and adversarial/red-team evaluation in software-only agents (see ai.safety.agent_red_team).
Robot manipulation / VLA evaluation benchmarks (LIBERO, SimplerEnv, RoboArena, CALVIN, RoboCasa). Excludes AI agent benchmarks (see ai.eval.agent_eval).

Robot-specific failure-mode analysis (RoboFAC, REFLECT-eval). Excludes general AI agent failure taxonomies (see ai.eval.failure_taxonomy) and online error recovery (see vla.reasoning.error_recovery_replanning).
